exemplar.title: What is the psychological impact of slavery on the characters of the novel Beloved by Toni Morrison?

detailedExemplarData.title: What is the psychological impact of slavery on the characters of the novel Beloved by Toni Morrison?

subject.title: English B
subject.description:
**IB English B** is a **Group 2** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **language acquisition through five prescribed themes: identities, experiences, human ingenuity, social organization, and sharing the planet**, with a strong focus on **developing receptive, productive, and interactive communication skills in English as a second language**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Productive skills - writing | 1 hour | 1 hour 15 minutes | 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Receptive skills - listening and reading comprehension | 1 hour 45 minutes | 2 hours | 50% |
| **Individual Oral** | Internal assessment - oral communication | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 25% |

strengths: Topic and purpose are communicated clearly and consistently throughout (title, abstract, introduction, and section headings).
Research question is explicitly stated, narrowly focused, and directly connected to each body section.
The investigation maintains a coherent thematic focus on psychological trauma and healing in Beloved.
weaknesses: Methodology lacks an explicit rationale for the selection and synthesis of secondary critics.
Criteria for source inclusion and the systematic approach to interpreting evidence are not fully articulated.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (topic communication and research question) clearly meet the 5â6 band descriptors, while methodology falls in the 3â4 band. Holistically, the investigation demonstrates effective and appropriate focus and question clarity but only a mostly complete method, warranting a final score of 5 out of 6.

strengths: Demonstrates clear and coherent subject knowledge of Morrisonâs novel and relevant literary-critical theories.
Terminology and concepts (e.g., generational trauma, purgation ritual) are used accurately and consistently.
Quotations from primary and secondary sources are generally well chosen to support thematic analysis.
weaknesses: Application of source material is occasionally superficial, inserting citations as confirmation rather than engaging them critically.
One claim about community impact lacks an in-text citation, weakening evidential support.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Subject knowledge and use of terminology both securely meet the 5â6 band criteria, while application of sources is mostly appropriate (band 3â4). Balancing these strands yields a holistic score of 5 out of 6.

strengths: Research is consistently relevant to the research question, drawing on both primary text and appropriately chosen secondary criticism.
Original insights (e.g., Stamp Paidâs duality) demonstrate nuanced critical thought beyond summary.
Argument is logically structured, with effective synthesis of character studies into a coherent conclusion.
weaknesses: Analysis occasionally slips into descriptive summary and would benefit from deeper engagement with counter-arguments.
Conclusion repeats earlier details rather than highlighting broader implications.
Limited deployment of contrasting critical perspectives reduces argumentative depth.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research quality meets the 7â9 band; analysis depth and discussion are mid-band but strengthened by original insights and coherent structure. Holistically this aligns with the upper end of the 7â9 band, justifying a score of 9 out of 12.

strengths: All standard structural elements are present and clearly ordered (title page, contents, labeled sections, works cited).
Layout conventions are correctly applied (word count, italics, numbering) and enhance readability.
weaknesses: Table of contents entries show minor misalignment and inconsistent indentation.
Heading styles vary across sections, and some paragraphs are overly long, affecting visual clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report fully satisfies the top band (3â4) descriptors for both structure and layout, with only minor formatting inconsistencies. This merits the maximum Presentation score of 4 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicitly stated in the preliminary metadata and repeated in the introduction.  It is narrowly framed around one novel and a single interpretive angle (psychological impact), avoiding the typical over-breadth of slavery as a whole.  Subsequent body sections map directly onto the RQ, showing an effective connection between question and discussion.  Minor wording repetition aside, the clarity and focus satisfy band 5â6 quality.

The essay moves beyond summary to analyse symbolism (milk, tobacco-tin), narrative structure, and inter-character dynamics, and it dialogues with scholarly views.  Still, portions are descriptive, and counter-positions are seldom weighed, creating some unevenness.  This reflects solid, middle-band analytical depth (4-6) rather than the consistently sophisticated reasoning demanded by bands 7-9 or 10-12.

Quotations from Morrison and seven scholarly articles are woven into every thematic section, and they are generally well chosen to illustrate points about trauma, motherhood, masculinity, and community rituals.  The majority of the material is relevant, though occasionally a citation is inserted as confirmation rather than being critically integrated.  This âmostlyâ appropriate application corresponds to band 3-4 rather than the fully assured utilisation required for band 5-6.

Layout follows EE conventions: word count, section headings, and works cited are formatted clearly; italics are used correctly for titles.  Minor inconsistencies (numbering in table of contents, occasional line-spacing issues) are present but do not impede readability, so performance meets the âcorrectly appliedâ criteria of the top presentation band.

The student presents a coherent summary of plot events, character back-stories, and critical theories (Freudian, communal ritual, generational trauma) that reflects a solid grasp of the novel and of relevant literary-critical discourse.  Explanations of Setheâs infanticide, Paul Dâs âtobacco-tinâ image, and Baby Suggsâs rituals show confident knowledge, fulfilling âclear and coherentâ expectations, though not reaching the exceptional insight that would merit the top of the band.

Standard EE structural elementsâtitle page, contents table, clearly labelled sections, conclusion, and works citedâare all present and sensibly ordered.  Headings methodically mirror the table of contents and the research trajectory.  The completeness and clarity satisfy the descriptors for band 3-4.

A range of primary textual evidence is paired with a judicious, if not exhaustive, selection of secondary criticism.  Each critic chosen (Schapiro, Moglen, Krumholz, etc.) directly addresses the psychological dimension of slavery, demonstrating clear relevance.  Although the bibliography is not extensive enough for the very highest level, the consistency and pertinence of research place it firmly in the lower end of the 7â9 band.

The studentâs title, abstract, and introduction communicate a clear and consistently maintained focus on âthe psychological impact of slaveryâ in Beloved.  Each section heading, quotation selection, and paragraph returns to this focus, demonstrating that the topic is not merely stated but actively drives the investigation.  The purposeâexploring trauma and healing in five key charactersâis appropriate for English B Category 3 and remains visible throughout, so the descriptor for accurate and effective communication is fulfilled.

A logical progressionâcharacter studies leading to synthesis in the conclusionâshows a reasoned argument.  The student evaluates motives (e.g., Stamp Paidâs âselfish selflessnessâ) and effects of rituals, but alternative interpretations are limited and some assertions rely heavily on a single critic.  Argumentation therefore achieves âeffectiveâ but not fully âfocused and coherentâ excellence, warranting mid-band marks.

A works-cited list of primary and secondary sources, together with references to how each critic will be used, indicates informed source selection.  However, the essay does not articulate a detailed research method (e.g., rationale for critical frameworks, criteria for source inclusion, or explanation of how interpretations were synthesized).  Because evidence of method is âmostly completeâ but not consistently explicit or systematic, the performance aligns with the upper end of band 3-4.

Literary and psychoanalytic terminologyâe.g., âgenerational trauma,â âpurgation ritual,â âpsychic death,â and âstream of consciousnessââis used accurately and consistently.  Citations are properly embedded, and key terms are defined or contextualised for clarity.  There are negligible misuses, so performance meets the top-band descriptor.

annotations.comment: The research question is split across lines and cut off mid-sentence due to a formatting breakâcorrect to maintain readability.
The Britannica entry lacks DOI or stable link formatting similar to other sourcesâensure uniform citation style.
The title is precise and clearly focuses on the investigationâs topic, effectively guiding the readerâs expectations.
Italicize the novel title Beloved in the essay title to adhere to MLA/APA conventions for literary works.
The research question is stated clearly at the start and aligns directly with the essayâs focus on psychological impact.
Including the word count confirms adherence to the 4 000-word limit requirement for HL Extended Essays.
Table of contents entries show minor misalignment and inconsistent indentation of section numbersâadjust for clarity.
The introduction includes extensive author biography that does not directly serve the research questionâstreamline to focus on psychological themes.
The opening paragraph provides broad context but could be tightened to foreground the research focus on trauma and healing.
Section headings closely mirror the table of contents and maintain a clear structureâthis consistency aids navigation.
Mentioning authors like Caesar and Schapiro without explaining selection criteria weakens the methodology sectionâadd a rationale for source inclusion.
The integration of Schapiroâs quotation is smooth and directly supports the argument on maternal trauma.
An inline MathML tag appears visibly (âdisplay="inline">-â); remove or replace with a proper dash character.
The section header level varies (h3 vs h4) in later partsâstandardize heading levels for uniformity.
The discussion of Paul Dâs tobacco-tin metaphor adeptly applies psychoanalytic concepts to literary analysis.
While Moglenâs interpretation is used effectively, consider introducing a contrasting critic to deepen critical analysis.
The portrayal of Stamp Paidâs identity crisis shows nuanced understanding of character motivation.
Several paragraphs exceed a page in length, which can overwhelm the readerâbreak into smaller units for clarity.
The analysis of Stamp Paidâs acts as both selfless and selfish is an original critical insight.
The second-level heading here uses h2 but earlier similar sections used h3âensure consistent heading styles.
The header âBaby Suggsâs healing ritualsâ should italicize the novel title when mentioned, maintaining consistency.
The integration of Freudian theory into discussion of Baby Suggsâs rituals demonstrates excellent subject knowledge.
Quoting the exorcism scene vividly supports the argument about communal healing rituals.
The final mention of community impact lacks an in-text citationâadd a reference to strengthen the claimâs credibility.
The conclusion effectively summarizes findings but largely repeats earlier detailsâconsider synthesizing to highlight broader implications.
The âWorks citedâ header is clear and correctly formatted, signaling a complete reference list.
Splitting the works-cited list across pages reduces continuityâconsolidate entries or clearly label continuation.
